title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It Wants to Go Nuclear.
summary: Opponents warn that a U.S.-Saudi deal could be a step toward the kingdom building an atomic bomb. But there are other reasons for the world’s largest oil exporter to diversify its energy mix.

title: Israel Welcomes Trump’s Demand for Diplomatic Ties With Saudi Arabia
summary: Israeli officials embraced the possibility of normalized ties, but analysts said the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal nonetheless remained bad for Israel.

title: Even as War Rages, Investors Bet Oil Will Be Cheaper in a Few Months
summary: Futures contracts reflect an expectation that low inventories and midterm elections are likely to push the Trump administration to try to ease tensions with Iran.

title: The Tate Brothers Are Losing Right-Wing Allies After Their Arrest
summary: After Andrew and Tristan Tate, prominent leaders of the so-called manosphere, were arrested in Miami, many of their onetime allies on the American right pulled back support.

title: Why I Sought Out a Taliban Commander I Had Fought
summary: First as a Marine and then as a journalist, I spent years tied to Afghanistan. I returned one last time to speak to a former enemy in search of answers, and perhaps closure.

title: The Devotion of Willow Smith
summary: The daughter of a Hollywood power couple, she has gone above and beyond to prove herself. Virtuosity and spiritual aspirations fill her latest, “The Thread.”

title: Trump Prepares Yet Another Strategy to Resurrect Global Tariffs
summary: The administration’s 10 percent global tariffs are set to expire at midnight, but officials have been preparing replacements.

title: Trump Administration Drops Subpoena Demanding List of Jews From Penn
summary: For more than a year, the Trump administration pressed the university for records about antisemitism complaints and a list of campus groups involved with Judaism.

title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

title: Saying ‘Google It’ Makes You Sound Old. ‘Search It Up’ Instead.
summary: As online research evolves, so has the language we use to describe it.

title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
summary: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.

title: F.D.A. Announces New Cyclosporiasis Outbreak With 72 Cases
summary: Health officials did not identify the location of the outbreak, or what they believed was causing it.

title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

title: Four Young Mathematicians Receive Prestigious Fields Medals
summary: The awards are bestowed only once every four years to mathematicians under the age of 40.

title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

title: Gen Z Fell Out of Love With Work. I Don’t Blame Them.
summary: For Gen Z college graduates, the way millennials lived out their ambitions no longer makes sense.

title: China’s A.I. Has a Winning Formula. America, Don’t Panic.
summary: Open-weight models have been the underlying strength of Chinese artificial intelligence strategy. America should take cues.

title: Photographic Evidence
summary: We look at how The Times uses photographs and video to better understand what’s happening in the war with Iran.

title: ‘We Will All Die From Ebola Here’: Virus Stalks Crowded Camps in Congo
summary: There are a million people displaced by violence in the epicenter. They live in dangerous conditions and are fighting the virus with very little clean water.
